global.title: Swarm feed topics follow ph:v2:<category>:<scope> naming convention
global.content:
# Swarm Feed Topic Naming Convention

All Powerhouse Swarm feeds follow the `ph:v2:<category>:<scope>` naming pattern.

## Single-user feed topics

- `ph:v2:user:<address>` — user's drive index
- `ph:v2:drive:<driveId>` — drive's document list + folders
- `ph:v2:doc:<docId>` — document's operation batch list
- `ph:v2:profile:<address>` — public profile (unencrypted)
- `ph:v2:share:<from>:<to>` — share manifest between users

## Collaboration feed topics

- `ph:v2:collab:<docId>` — collaboration index (who's in)
- `ph:v2:ops:<address>:<docId>` — per-user operation stream

## Pattern rules

- Every feed has exactly one owner writing, anyone can read
- Collaboration is modeled as N users, each with their own ops feed
- The collab index feed tells everyone where to look
- `v2` indicates schema version (future-proofing)
global.description: Swarm feed topics use ph:v2 prefix with category (user, drive, doc, collab, ops, profile, share) and scope identifier to uniquely name each feed.
